title: Joy Is Not Something I Have to Earn
subtitle: An affirmation for taking pleasure without first completing the list
excerpt: A short affirmation for uncoupling joy from productivity and completion. For the pattern of postponing pleasure until the conditions are right: the work is done, the house is clean, the obligations are met.

content.text: A short affirmation for uncoupling joy from the condition of having earned it. The belief that joy is a reward for completion (for work done, obligations met, the list finished) keeps pleasure perpetually just ahead of wherever you currently are. The list is never finished. The conditions are never quite right. This affirmation drops the requirement.
Say it three times. It works best said before doing something enjoyable, not after:
Joy is not something I have to earn. It is available to me right now, as I am.

Two sentences. The first removes the condition. The second makes joy a present-tense availability, not a future reward. Both clauses matter: removing the earning requirement, and establishing that the access is now, not when.
